theme_and_archetype.rationale: The dashboard needs to feel serious, secure, trustworthy, and dense-information-friendly. It focuses on authority, clarity, and function-first design. We are avoiding the standard 'soft SaaS' look by using stark contrast, strict grids, and flat geometry.

rules.gradients: BANNED. Use flat solid colors only.
rules.shadows: BANNED. Rely on 1px solid borders for component separation.

layout_and_spacing.borders: 1px solid border-gray-200 on cards, tables, and inputs
layout_and_spacing.navigation: Strict top navigation bar with a secondary sub-navigation row below it for sections (Overview, Connectors, Logs, Security). This maximizes horizontal space for data tables.

shadcn_overrides.Button: Remove border-radius (rounded-none). Primary button must use bg-[#002FA7]. Use flat design without shadows.
shadcn_overrides.Card: Remove border-radius and shadow. Add border-gray-200. Use bg-white.
shadcn_overrides.Table: Dense padding (py-2 px-3). Use IBM Plex Mono for IDs, timestamps, and IPs. Hover state should be a stark bg-gray-50.
shadcn_overrides.Dialog: Remove border-radius. Add a solid 1px border. Backdrop should be a dense backdrop-blur-sm bg-gray-900/40.
shadcn_overrides.Input: Remove border-radius. Focus state should be a stark 2px solid ring of #002FA7 (ring-0 focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-[#002FA7] focus-visible:ring-offset-0 focus-visible:border-transparent).
shadcn_overrides.Badge: Flat backgrounds, stark text colors, rounded-none.

custom_elements.copy_chip: A small flat button with the value and a copy icon. When copied, show a brief success state (icon change) and a Sonner toast.
custom_elements.one_time_secret_reveal: A stark warning box (border-l-4 border-warning bg-warning/10) containing the secret in a monospace font. A prominent 'I have saved it' button to dismiss.

login.layout: Split screen (50/50). Left: Abstract geometric visual. Right: Centered login form.
login.visuals: Left side image: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1595411425732-e69c1abe2763?crop=entropy&cs=srgb&fm=jpg&ixid=M3w4NjA2ODl8MHwxfHNlYXJjaHwyfHxhYnN0cmFjdCUyMGdlb21ldHJpYyUyMHBhdHRlcm4lMjBibGFjayUyMGFuZCUyMHdoaXRlfGVufDB8fHx8MTc4MzA4MDUxNnww&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&q=85 (Black and white abstract texture).
login.action: Emergent Google Auth button (Stark white button with Google logo and black border).

dashboard_shell.nav: Top Navbar with Logo (Razio Connect), User Avatar/Logout. Sub-nav with links: Overview, Connectors, Sync Logs, Security Events.

overview.layout: 4-column grid (col-span-1 each) for high-level metrics. Use stark typography for numbers (e.g., text-4xl font-black).

connectors.actions: 'Create Connector' button (Primary color). Table with columns: Name, Company, Status, Last Sync, Actions (Regenerate, Revoke). Use DropdownMenu for row actions.

logs_and_security.layout: Filters (Connector dropdown, Date Range, Status) in a horizontal bar above the table. Table rows must be expandable to reveal raw JSON request/response in a monospace block.

login_background.description: Black and white geometric abstraction for the left pane of the login screen.

technical_requirements.testing: All interactive elements MUST have a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='create-connector-btn', data-testid='copy-secret-btn').
technical_requirements.state_management: Use React state for minimal UI state. Fetch data from backend (assumed to be implemented or mocked for now).

instructions_to_main_agent: Load Google Fonts in the main index.html or index.css.
Initialize Sonner Toaster in the root layout.
Implement the Layout component with the Top Nav and Sub Nav.
Build out the Login page using the split-screen design and provided image.
Implement the Dashboard views (Overview, Connectors, Logs, Security). Mock the data if backend endpoints are not ready.
Ensure the Create Connector flow includes the one-time secret reveal UI.
Strictly adhere to the 'rounded-none' and 'no-shadow' rules in Tailwind configuration and Shadcn overrides.
Implement expandable table rows for the Sync Logs to view JSON payloads.
Use standard fetch API or axios to interact with /api/tally/sync, /api/tally/logs, etc., wrapping them in try-catch to display Sonner toasts on error.
